reasoning: The "No to a 10 Million Switzerland" referendum (scheduled for June 2026) faces the typical headwinds of Swiss popular initiatives, which fail 85-90% of the time, especially when opposed by the Federal Council and Parliament. Early polling (LeeWas) showed support declining from 48% in November 2025 to 45% in March 2026. Because support for popular initiatives almost universally decreases during the campaign period due to status quo bias and cross-party opposition (including business groups warning about risks to the EU free movement agreement), an initiative polling below 50% three months before the vote is highly likely to fail. Although the polling firm Sotomo previously suggested it had a chance due to the high salience of immigration, the recent downward trend to 45% firmly places this on a trajectory to lose, likely earning 35-40% on vote day. The Manifold market was around 25% in early April, but fundamental reference class forecasting and the March polling suggest a slightly lower probability is appropriate.
